text: I’m checking the app structure, Next.js version, and existing rendering configuration first. Since this is a Next.js change, I’ll also consult the installed Next.js docs before editing anything.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!*node_modules*'"' -g 'package.json' -g 'next.config.*' -g 'app/**' -g 'pages/**' -g 'AGENTS.md' | head -200 && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' package.json && printf '\\n--- next config candidates ---\\n' && for f in next.config.js next.config.mjs next.config.ts; do [ -f \""'$f" ] && { echo "### $f"; sed -n '"'1,220p' \""'$f"; }; done'

text: The app already has the shape partial pre-rendering is meant to support: a static shell with two async product/recommendation sections behind `Suspense`. I’m verifying the canary’s current Cache Components/PPR API and build requirements now, because the configuration and opt-in semantics changed across Next versions.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n -i \"partial|pre-render|cacheComponents|cache components|dynamicIO|Suspense|use cache\" node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/06-cache-components.mdx node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference 2>/dev/null | head -240 && printf '\\n--- cache components guide ---\\n' && sed -n '1,280p' node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/06-cache-components.mdx && printf '\\n--- config type references ---\\n' && rg -n \"cacheComponents|experimental.*ppr|ppr\" node_modules/next/dist/docs node_modules/next/dist/server node_modules/next/dist -g '*.d.ts' -g '*.mdx' 2>/dev/null | head -200"

text: The installed Next 16 docs confirm that `cacheComponents: true` is the current unified opt-in and that it includes Partial Prerendering; the older experimental `ppr` flag is no longer the right setting. I’m adding that single config option, then I’ll run the production build to catch any uncached work that needs an explicit boundary.

text: The production build passes and reports `/` as `◐ (Partial Prerender)`, with the static shell plus dynamic server-streamed content. Next also auto-updated `tsconfig.json` to include its generated dev types during the build, so I’m checking the final diff to ensure only expected changes remain.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "git status --short && git diff -- next.config.ts tsconfig.json && printf '\\n--- config ---\\n' && sed -n '1,120p' next.config.ts && printf '\\n--- tsconfig ---\\n' && sed -n '1,180p' tsconfig.json"

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "printf '%s\\n' '--- next.config.ts ---' && sed -n '1,120p' next.config.ts && printf '%s\\n' '--- tsconfig.json ---' && sed -n '1,180p' tsconfig.json && printf '%s\\n' '--- generated files ---' && find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \\( -name 'tsconfig.json' -o -name 'next.config.ts' \\) -print"

text:
Enabled Partial Prerendering via `cacheComponents` in [next.config.ts](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts:4).

Verified with `npm run build`: `/` is reported as `◐ Partial Prerender`, with static HTML and dynamic streamed content. Next also added `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` to `tsconfig.json` during the build.
